name: Hasin ibn Numayr
title: Commander of Archers
shortDescription: A senior Umayyad commander present in Karbala who later commanded the siege and destruction of the Kaaba.
fullStory:
Husayn ibn Numayr (Hasin) was a loyal commander to the Umayyad dynasty with a long history of enmity towards the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

In Kufa, he was the commander of Ibn Ziyad's police forces and was tasked with closing the routes leading to Kufa. He was the one who arrested Qays ibn Musahir (the Imam's messenger). In Karbala, he commanded part of the cavalry and archers of Umar Sa'd's army.

His crimes didn't end in Karbala. After Yazid's death, Hasin ibn Numayr attacked Mecca and, to suppress Abdullah ibn Zubayr, bombarded the Kaaba with catapults, causing a fire in the House of God.

He spent his entire life serving oppressors and was ultimately killed alongside Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad in the Battle of Khazir by Ibrahim ibn Malik al-Ashtar's army.
quote: (His order in Umayyad wars:) Target the tents and houses with fire...
